Q1 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding the term “Sin Tax” mentioned in the recent 56th GST Council meet:
1. It is an excise tax specifically levied on certain goods deemed harmful to society, such as tobacco, alcohol and other similar products.
2. The primary purpose is to increase the cost of these goods to discourage their consumption.
3. The GST Council has recommended a maximum special rate of 28% for all sin and luxury goods.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• The 56th GST Council meeting focused on rate rationalisation to benefit the common man. It
introduced a special 40% rate for luxury items while slashing rates on hygiene products (soaps, shampoos) to 5%.
• Crucially, it exempted all life and health insurance policies and set zero GST for 33 life-saving drugs.
• While the standard high slab is 28%, the 56th GST Council meeting recommended a special
rate of 40% for specific sin and luxury goods.

Q3 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which among the following statements best describes the term “Stablecoins”?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Stablecoins are cryptocurrencies designed to maintain price stability by pegging their value to
underlying assets such as Fiat currencies (e.g., USD, Euro), Commodities (e.g., gold), other
cryptocurrencies, or Algorithm-based systems.

Q4 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding the Index of Industrial Production (IIP):
1. It is an index which shows the growth rates in different industry groups of the economy in a stipulated period of time.
2. It is released by the Central Statistical Organisation (CSO) on a monthly basis.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• The Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) is set to revise the base
year of the Index of Industrial Production (IIP) to 2022–23, aligning it with the new base year
proposed for GDP estimation.
• The IIP is an index which shows the growth rates in different industry groups of the economy
in a stipulated period of time.
• It is released by the Central Statistical Organisation (CSO), Ministry of Statistics and Programme
Implementation.

Q5 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The PM–SETU scheme, launched by Union Government, has the objective of:

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• The Prime Minister launched the Pradhan Mantri Skilling and Employability Transformation
through Upgraded ITIs (PM – SETU).
• It is a centrally sponsored scheme to transform 1,000 Government ITIs across India into
modern, industry-aligned training institutions.
• PM-SETU will follow a hub-and-spoke model, with 200 hub ITIs linked to 800 spoke ITIs.

Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• The ’21’ in Agenda 21 refers to the 21st century.
• Agenda 21 is a non-binding action plan of the United Nations with regard to sustainable development.
• It is a product of the Earth Summit (UN Conference on Environment and Development) held
in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil, in 1992.

The phenomenon of increasing concentration of a substance, such as a toxic chemical, in the tissues of tolerant organisms at successively higher levels in a food chain is known as:

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Biomagnification, also known as bioamplification or biological magnification, is the increasing
concentration of a substance, such as a toxic chemical, in the tissues of tolerant organisms at
successively higher levels in a food chain.

Q27 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding Edge Computing:
1. Unlike Cloud Computing, which centralizes data processing in remote data centers, Edge Computing processes data at the periphery of the network.
2. It is particularly essential for applications requiring ultra-low latency, such as autonomous vehicles and robot-assisted surgery.
3. Edge Computing can guarantee response times and operate independently of continuous internet connectivity.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Instead of sending all data to distant cloud servers for processing, edge computing brings the
processing power closer to where data originates thus providing ultra-low latency. Thus, all the
given statements are correct.

Q28 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. Cookies are small files of information that a web server generates and sends to a web browser.
2. Cookies help inform websites about the user, enabling the websites to personalize the user experience.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
A cookie is a small block of data created by a web server while a user is browsing a website.
Web browsers store the cookies they receive for a predetermined period of time, or for the length
of a user’s session on a website. They attach the relevant cookies to any future requests the user
makes of the web server. For example, ecommerce websites use cookies to know what merchandise
users have placed in their shopping carts. In addition, some cookies are necessary for security
purposes, such as authentication cookies.

Q32 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions (MIME) is an extension of the original Simple Mail Transport Protocol (SMTP) Email protocol.
2. MIME can reject mail that exceeds a certain size, but there is no word limit in Simple Mail Transport Protocol (SMTP).
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Simple Mail Transfer Protocol (SMTP) is a protocol that ensures that an email is successfully
delivered from the sender’s server to the recipient’s server. Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions
(MIME), however, is an extension of SMTP. It lets users exchange multimedia content, including
audio, video, images, and applications, over email.
SMTP can reject mail that exceeds a certain size, but there is no word limit in MIME.

hardware works correctly, initialize the hardware components, load the operating system, and
preparing the computer for use.

Q34 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following is/are the benefits of applying blockchain technology in any industry?
1. Greater Transparency
2. Increased Efficiency
3. Better Security
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Blockchain is a type of data-structure ledger that records owners/participants information. They
are decentralised in nature and thus can stores transactional accords. Thus, Blockchain provides
transparency through shared ledgers, increases efficiency by removing intermediaries, and
enhances security via immutable, decentralized encryption.
Advantages:
• Better security
• Efficient
• Transparent
Applications:
• Banking sector
• Capital sector
• Healthcare agricultural etc.

Insufficient knowledge, incorrect information and memory blocks are belonging to which type of barrier to creative thinking?

Solution

(b)

fear or risk taking, motivation.
Intellectual block: Arises from a poor choice of the problem solving strategy, insufficient
knowledge base.
Environmental block: Arises from immediate physical environment or social environment,
criticism.
Cultural block: Set of thought pattern from living in a culture.

Q49 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

In NDT, eddy current test is used to detect

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Eddy current test detects crack, hardness conductivity and also checks dimensions. It is also used
to soft materials. The magnitude of this test depends upon electrical conductivity of metals, size
and shape of specimens, hardness of microstructure and the amount and type of defects in specimen.
This test evaluates range of products including round and irregularly shaped objects.

The five project management process groups are based on which of the following quality improvement cycles?

Correct answer: C

Do → Executing
Check → Monitoring & Controlling
Act → Closing/Improvements

Q53 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements regarding social cost is/are correct?
1. Social cost refers to the cost arising from the production of a commodity that is not explicitly borne by the firm.
2. Social cost can be classified into private cost and external cost.
3. External costs are directly paid by the producer of the commodity.
4. Environmental damage caused by a project is an example of social cost.
Select the correct answer using the codes given below

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
About social cost:
• Social cost is the cost imposed on society and not directly borne by the firm.
• Social cost = Private cost + External cost.
1. External costs are not directly paid by the producer; they are borne by society.
2. Environmental damage (pollution, deforestation, waste emission) is a classic social cost.

Q61 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. An alloy of copper, tin and zinc is known as Gun metal.
2. German silver is an alloy of nickel, copper and zinc.
3. A cold chisel is made of tempered steel.
Which of the above statements is/are true?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• Gunmetal, also known as red brass is an alloy of copper, tin, and zin (typically composed of 88% copper, 8–10% tin, and 2–4% zinc).
• A cold chisel is a tool made of tempered steel used for cutting ‘cold’ metals.
• German silver is a copper alloy with nickel and often zinc. The usual formulation is 60% copper, 15% nickel and 25% zinc. German silver is named for its silvery appearance, but it contains no elemental silver unless plated.

Q64 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Main objective of ‘normalising’ a steel specimen is

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Normalizing aims to refine the grain structure, enhance mechanical properties, and alleviate internal
stresses within the material. It produces a more uniform and fine-grained microstructure, improving
strength and toughness.

Q65 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A reversible change in the atomic structure of steel with corresponding change in the properties is known as

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Allotropy is the property of some chemical elements to exist in two or more different forms, in
the same physical state, known as allotropes.
A reversible change in the atomic structure of steel with corresponding change in the properties
is known as allotropic change.

Q66 Not checked

Hall effect is utilised to find

Correct answer: A
Solution

Q67 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A p-type semiconductor can be obtained by doping silicon or germanium with

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Boron is a trivalent impurity. When added to silicon or germanium, it creates holes as majority carriers, producing a p-type semiconductor. Whereas Arsenic (As), Antimony (Sb) and Phosphorus (P) are all Group 15 elements (Pentavalent) and thus, produces n-type semiconductor.

Q68 Not checked

Not attempted

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

Q71 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1: Ceramics are used extensively in industry because of their low electrical resistance.
S2: Cermets are combination of metals and ceramics, bonded together.
Which of the following is correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Ceramics have high electrical resistance and are extensively used in industry. Hence, statement S1 is false.

Q72 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
Electrets are the materials which are
1. having permanent electric moments
2. electromagnets
3. very similar to permanent magnet materials
4. similar to anti-ferroelectric materials
Which of these statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Electrets are the class of dielectric material containing quasi-permanent electric charge or molecular
dipoles, which can generate internal and external electric fields. They are the electrostatic equivalent
of a permanent magnet that can store charges for prolonged time, as long as 100 years.

Q91 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is NOT the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): A tornado diagram is a graphical output of comparative sensitivity analysis and is commonly used in project risk analysis.
Statement (II): It represents the impact of variation of one input variable at a time on the project outcome, arranged in descending order of influence.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Tornado diagram is a special bar chart used in sensitivity and risk analysis. Variables are varied
one at a time, and their impacts are ranked, giving the characteristic tornado shape.

Statement (II): MBNQA Award given for any organization or any individual person.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

organisation service sector, educational sector etc.

Correct answer: D

Q94 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is NOT the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): Simple Mail Transfer Protocol (SMTP) is a set of communication guidelines that allow the software to transmit an electronic mail over the internet.
Statement (II): It is a program used for sending messages to other computer users based on email addresses.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
SMTP defines a process for exchanging data between an email client and a mail server. It uses e-mail addresses to deliver messages to other computer users. Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is NOT the correct explanation of Statement (I).

Q95 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is NOT the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): Operating system provides a basis for application programs and acts as an intermediary between the computer user and computer hardware.
Statement (II): Data management is not a basic function of operating system as it is carried out by the higher-level software applications and databases.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
An operating system (OS) is the software that manages the hardware and software resources of
a computer. It acts as an intermediary between the computer hardware and the applications that
run on the computer. The operating system provides the necessary support and services to run
applications, manage memory, handle input/output operations, and perform other tasks. Database
management is not a basic function of operating system. Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are
individually true but Statement (II) is NOT the correct explanation of Statement (I).

Q97 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is NOT the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): The primary objective of the Rashtriya Gokul Mission (RGM) is the development and scientific conservation of indigenous bovine breeds.
Statement (II): The mission aims to enhance the productivity of these native breeds, increase milk production sustainably, and promote genetic upgradation.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• The primary objective of the Rashtriya Gokul Mission (RGM) is the development and scientific conservation of indigenous bovine breeds such as Gir, Sahiwal, and Red Sindhi.
• The mission aims to enhance the productivity of these native breeds, increase milk production sustainably, and promote genetic upgradation.
• It supports farmers through advanced breeding techniques, artificial insemination, and setting up specialized breeding centers called Gokul Grams.

(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.

Statement (II): Act Utilitarianism is sometimes referred to as consequentialism.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Utilitarianism ignores motivation and focuses only on consequences. This leaves many people
unsatisfied. Hence statement (I) is incorrect.

Statement (I): In the parasitic food chain, the pyramids are always upright.
Statement (II): In the parasitic food chain, a single plant may support the growth of many herbivores and each herbivore in turn may provide nutrition to several parasites.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
In the parasitic food chain, the pyramids are always inverted.
